TextENGNew: ...Just as Tomoe was about to reach a stopping point in her work and thinking about going out for lunch, Yamaoki suddenly called her in.
"You need something, Chief?"
"Yes... well, have a seat."
"...Thank you."
Yamaoki was standing by the window and, on his prompting, Tomoe took a seat on the sofa.
There was a beautiful potted orchid sitting on the reception desk, giving the room a lovely aroma.
(That girl always loved gardening and flowers like these.)
Tomoe gently touched a drooping bundle of flowers while thinking that.

The soft and silky-smooth texture of the petals immediately brought a smile to her face, but... seeing Yamaoki from behind across the room, she readjusted herself and began to don a serious expression.
Yamaoki gazed out the window and smoked a cigarette with his back still turned toward Tomoe.
...Sensing the uncomfortable atmosphere, Tomoe lightly loosened her collar. This felt just like the situation half a year ago.
(Is this another transfer discussion?@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1743. ...But the Chief shouldn't have any authority over me at the moment.)

In which case, did someone make a protest or file a complaint against her? Or maybe this is a reprimand? ...So many thoughts were running through her head, she wouldn't be surprised no matter what came of it.
(I've gotten a whole lot more brazen.)
Thinking that, an impulsive laugh welled up inside her.
When she was sent away from Kakiuchi and pulled off of detective work, she was seriously thinking about retiring thanks to the disappointment and tragedy she endured.
But... not even a hint of that sentiment remained today.
If this was a similar request to resign, it wouldn't be too late to die fighting. ...Thanks to that open minded way of thinking, Tomoe was able to broaden her horizons and achieve peace of mind.
"Sorry to keep you waiting.@k@vS14/30/VT4Bb_yamaoki1004. ...Now, let's get to the point."
"Right."
So, what was coming? ...And how should she react to it?
This was Yamaoki. He was probably expecting Tomoe to be shocked or to shout.
In which case, maybe it would be more fun if she quietly accepted whatever it was...?
Tomoe couldn't help but giggle as those wicked thoughts came to mind.
"...?@k@vS14/30/VT4Bb_yamaoki1006. Is something wrong, Minai-kun?"
"No, not really.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1747. Sorry, what was it you needed from me?"
"Mmm, the truth is..."
Yamaoki cut himself off there, then picked up the bundle of clipped together documents on his desk and held it out to Tomoe.
Tomoe respectfully accepted it, and without saying a word she turned over the cover and read the text... but...
Her composure until just a moment ago was completely blown away, and her eyes were open wide in shock.
"Wh... what is this...?!"
"Hmm... I must admit, I was feeling a little lost about if I should ask you this, but I can't say that's the case anymore.@k@vS14/30/VT4Bb_yamaoki1009. ...So here it is."
"C-come on... This... this is...!"
She quivering.
changeReason: New text 'She quivering' has grammatical error (missing 'was'). Original is correct.

TextENGNew: Then, Tomoe's shoulders shuddered when she saw the pile laying on the desk.
"You can't ask me for something like thiiiiiiiiis!!!"
Tomoe slammed the documents down hard enough to make the potted plant shake, and violently cried out.
Spelled out there in old-fashioned messy lettering were words like "wedding" and "announcement."
And right beside the documents were two massive piles of postcards.
...In other words, he was asking Tomoe to do calligraphy work, writing invitations for the wedding and the reception.
And of course, there was no need to say whose wedding it was. What's worse, the note at the end that said "clean copy please♪" circled in red pen further aroused her bloodlust.

"And more importantly, what's with this date?!@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1752. I did agree to you and Madoka getting married, but isn't this way too soon for the ceremony?!@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1753. No matter what you say, it's way too early!!"

"Mmm, but by having the ceremony in June it makes her a June Bride, which is well regarded as a symbol of happiness, is it not?@k@vS14/30/VT4Bb_yamaoki1011. If we miss this opportunity, we'll have to wait until next year."
"Incidentally, we've already missed the opportunity to have it on Lucky Day, so I feel that even if it's overdoing it a bit it's still worthwhile."

"This isn't overdoing it a bit, it's going way above and beyond!@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1755. It doesn't matter how much you want a June Bride! It's already June!@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1756. The wedding is less than a month out?!"

It was impressive that the people involved in the ceremony could accomodate that. Considering the timing, there was no need for them to accept a customer demand like that. In fact they wouldn't even be blamed for rejecting it as something outright insane.
...No, wait a second.
Could it be...? Did Yamaoki use his position with the police to pressure them into it?!
(He wouldn't go that far... No, I wouldn't put it past him!)
After all, last time he was suspected of accepting gifts he shut the whole thing down by saying "I am the rulebook." He didn't quite invoke the name of God, but just saying that is beyond rational.
Words like ethics and common sense are simply deleted from the dictionary when it's convenient. ...That was how the man named Kaoru Yamaoki operated!!
"Why did you bother with making your own invitations in the first place?!@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1759. Doesn't the wedding venue have copy staff you could ask to do this for you?!"

"Oh no, one of the conditions specified by the venue was that we create the invitations ourselves.@k@vS14/30/VT4Bb_yamaoki1014. After all, some concessions of that kind are unavoidable.@k@vS14/30/VT4Bb_yamaoki1015. But, bringing in another contractor just to do the work would incur ridiculous expenses... don't you agree?"

"Can an old man who lives in a mansion really be that stingy?!@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1761. Plus, even if you and I are cordial in the line of duty, in private we're more like mortal enemies!@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1762. Just what on earth are you thinking?!"
"Oh, is that so...?@k@vS14/30/VT4Bb_yamaoki1017. But Madoka-kun said you agreed to support our marriage..."
"Even if I support the marriage, that doesn't mean I'm cheering it on!"
She howling like a tiger so fiercely that her voice slipped into her dialect. No good, Tomoe.
changeReason: New text 'She howling' has grammatical error (missing 'was'). Original is correct.

TextENGNew: Why did he have to go so far pouring salt on the wound? It was like he was sprinkling it on to purify something!!
(Besides, is it normal to ask the bride's family to write the invitations?)
With blood running to her head and feeling a little dizzy, Tomoe barely held back the hostility bordering on murderous intent.
Was he that lacking in common sense to suggest this idea? Or was he intentionally harassing her? Or maybe both?!
"Even so, take a good look at this writing.@k@vS14/30/VT4Bb_yamaoki1019. My handwriting is so difficult to decipher it's like it's written in code. Even Madoka-kun had a hard time understanding it."

"Try to be at least a little embarrassed!@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1766. If you're that aware of the problem, wouldn't you be better off investing the time to improve your penmanship in the first place?!"
"Well, I've always thought about that.@k@vS14/30/VT4Bb_yamaoki1021. But somehow, it always got put off... and it only ever comes up at the moment I need it."
"In other words, by the time I need it it's already too late... it's the "after carnival" phenomenon."
"Stop with the terrible English, old man!@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1768. Playing dumb is seriously pissing me off!!"

"However, there is also a strong tendency across the world to make assumptions about one's intellect based on the beauty of the writing.@k@vS14/30/VT4Bb_yamaoki1024. Being the chief of this station, that may cast doubt on my capabilities."

"So, I was wondering what I could do about that, and that's when I remembered you.@k@vS14/30/VT4Bb_yamaoki1026. You took calligraphy... and made it to first level?@k@vS14/30/VT4Bb_yamaoki1027. No, was it second level?"
"Well, even if that wasn't the case, I hear you've become a master of word processing lately.@k@vS14/30/VT4Bb_yamaoki1029. ...Would it be so bad?"
"Yes I made it to second level in calligraphy!@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1770. And before you go around asking things like "would it be so bad?" you should start worrying about some fundamental things!!"
The blood vessels on Tomoe's forehead were about to burst, and her fists were raised in the air and shaking.
...However, Yamaoki dismissed her rage-filled voice with a calm expression, and handed the documents on the reception to desk over to Tomoe once again.

"Well, that was just a light introduction... but the main subject is on the next page.@k@vS14/30/VT4Bb_yamaoki1031. I understand that a young woman may be stunned by the flashy photos, but please take a look."
"Huuh?@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1772. ...You even want me to choose the wedding riiiing?!"
Behind the blank paper was a thick stack of color pages that seemed to be cut out of a booklet. And there, were many colorful photos of vibrant rings and glittering jewels.

And what's more, just because they had the words "engagement" and "wedding" attached, the design and the price were over the top... Tomoe's heart was racing so fast it felt like she was going to faint from rage.

"Well, at first, I was thinking of choosing for myself.@k@vS14/30/VT4Bb_yamaoki1033. However... Madoka-kun can be rather picky about the accessories she likes, so she won't be happy just because I got her something expensive."
"So, I thought it would be best to consult with a relative familiar with her tastes..."
"...Are you mocking me?@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1774. I knew it. You're making fun of me, aren't you Chief?@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1775. I'll seriously tear you limb from limb, all right?"

"Aha, no need to worry.@k@vS14/30/VT4Bb_yamaoki1036. If you see something in there you like too, consider it a commission fee. I'll see what I can do to take care of it.@k@vS14/30/VT4Bb_yamaoki1037. Choose anything you like."
"Seriously?!@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1777. I'd prefer a nut salvaged from a construction site over a gift from you!!"
"Oh, that's fine then.@k@vS14/30/VT4Bb_yamaoki1039. And then you'll run out into the road and shout "I won't die" to threaten our marriage?"
"That's a declaration!@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1779. Not a threat!!@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1780. The implication is completely different!!"
There was not even a hint of romance in this conversation, it was headache inducing.
A ring being given from a man to a woman has a special meaning in the first place. It didn't matter how expensive or what brand it was, there was no way she could accept something like this.
However, though still feeling enraged, she turned her eyes toward the catalog once again... and she felt just a little fascinated by the radiant jewelry.
Though she didn't have a man in her life, Tomoe was in fact an ordinary woman. She couldn't quite say she wasn't interested.
However, she continued to have old-fashioned ideas in that regard, so she still yearned for someone to give her a present like that.
(A ring, huh...?)
Tomoe had never been in a deep enough relationship with anyone to receive one. She always rejected the opportunity in order to pursue her own goals.
...Still, there may come a day when she's achieved her purpose, and then she might finally encounter an opportunity like that.
Although, she wasn't confident that day would ever actually come...
"Anyway, which of these do you think Madoka-kun would be most interested in? This one? This one? Or this one?@k@vS14/30/VT4Bb_yamaoki1041. Please tell me."
"I don't know.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1784. Maybe you should just close your eyes and pick one at random?"
"Preposterous!@k@vS14/30/VT4Bb_yamaoki1043. Take this seriously and choose one!!"
"You goddamn moron!@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1786. You seriously want me to choose!?!"
...This back and forth exchange was like a comedy duo.
But strangely enough, was it showing signs of growth that the unpleasant hostile feelings were no longer flaring up like they used to? Or was it a product of compromise?
Even Tomoe, herself, wasn't truly sure.
Anyway, that back and forth question and answer exchange continued for a while longer.
In the end, of course, Tomoe couldn't stand Yamaoki looking down on her with that miserable face, so she had no choice but to make a few suggestions to him.
She wasn't particularly confident on the subject of fashion, but she was able to make a few small recommendations regarding Madoka's tastes based on the time they lived together.
However, there was no guarantee that her tastes from back before she left home were still the same as today...
"Oh my, you've been a great help.@k@vS14/30/VT4Bb_yamaoki1045. ...Are you really okay without your share?"
"I'm fine.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1788. ...So, was that all you wanted to ask of me?@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1789. If that's the case, it's about time for me to..."
"...How is the progress on your investigation?"
Tomoe had already begun to stand up from the sofa, but she couldn't come up with a quick reply so she remained there, half-sitting, staring at Yamaoki.
...He took a seat on the sofa and gathered his documents, while turning to her with the same easy-going face as before.
(...I see, so that's why he called me here.)
Tomoe bitterly smiled to herself at this roundabout way of bringing her here.
Dispelling the tension and alertness with a completely unrelated conversation was probably his way of demonstrating he had no hostility or ill will... but that seemed a bit overly considerate.
Even if he hadn't done that, Tomoe trusted Yamaoki. ...It's just that she couldn't express it due to various circumstances.
Yamaoki just asked about "the investigation." It was clear he wasn't referring to the joint headquarters investigation she was involved with as a public relations officer.
He must've been referring to the secret investigation she was conducting with Fujita and the other detectives.
(Was it Fujita-kun who told him? Or maybe Hanada-kun...?)
But he didn't ask them to stop, so it didn't feel right to blame them.
In fact, their own circumstances were their top priority, so she warned them they could drop it any time if they needed to.
...Still, in a rough sense, Yamaoki had tolerated Tomoe and her cohorts' actions up until now.
And the reason he brought her here to ask about it... was because they had made some progress? Or maybe something went wrong?

"...I'm sorry for not letting you know you about this.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1795. I understand what a breach of authority it is for National Police Agency staff to assign work to precinct detectives."
"Absolutely.@k@vS14/30/VT4Bb_yamaoki1048. And not only that, but it's disrespectful to joint investigation headquarters."
"...So, could it be that someone lodged a complaint?@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1797. If that's the case, I should bear the responsibility alone. Please be gentle with Fujita-kun and the others..."

"Hahaha, no need to jump to conclusions.@k@vS14/30/VT4Bb_yamaoki1050. Neither Yamamura nor I have any intention of blaming you for it.@k@vS14/30/VT4Bb_yamaoki1051. And it seems that nobody else in the station has noticed your activities."
"...Thank goodness."
Tomoe put a hand on her chest as she breathed a sigh of relief.
...Unlike Tomoe, Fujita and Hanada had families to worry about. There was nothing admirable about letting them get punished.
"...However."
"Yes?"
"You're involved in a large-scale effort with investigation HQ that even extends as far as NRIPS."

"If you explain the situation to the higher-ups, it'd be possible to act as an investigator. Plus, you're on good terms with the prefectural police.@k@vS14/30/VT4Bb_yamaoki1055. Wouldn't it be more efficient and convenient to act through them?"
"Yes... in fact, that's what I was planning to do at first.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1801. ...However."
Tomoe sat down on the sofa once again and seemed to resign herself to speaking frankly. Fully explaining the situation would be necessary to gauge Yamaoki's true intentions.

"Apparently, there's reason to believe that our investigation content is being leaked.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1803. In fact, over the last six months the attitude of our sources toward us has drastically changed either just before or after the leaks, so there were times it hindered us from gathering information."
"Hmm... what evidence do you have of this?"

"Right here.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1805. This is the content of the questions that were defined at the time of creating the inquiry appointment, and this is the statement at the time of the actual inquiry.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1806. ...Clearly, this is evidence that the information we needed was being obstructed by a third party."
Tomoe spread out her police notebook and explained all the shorthand in her notes to Yamaoki.
Officials were attempting to secretly approach people connected to Reina Ryuugu and Nagisa Ozaki.
Like the truck driver responsible for Nagisa's fatal accident.
Or hospital officials working at the facility where Youko Hiranuma was suspected of killing a male student who was hospitalized there...

In all cases, the people questioned offered prepared answers like they already knew the content of the questions, and they'd always say they "don't remember" in with regards to the most important information.
And, on top of that...
"We identified a woman who was a close friend of Youko Hiranuma during her nursing school days.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1808. ...And at the moment, she seems to be missing."
The woman in question apparently met with Youko Hiranuma a few days before the incident, and they talked about various things.

The investigator heard this from one of her work colleagues and immediately tried to contact her... but when they tried visiting her home, she was already gone, allegedly leaving on a trip a few days prior.

"Nevertheless, when they received a warrant and searched the house, they found her bankbook and signature stamp were still in the house.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1810. ...It was hard to imagine she'd go on a long trip and not bring those with her."
"...Indeed."
"What's more, her destination remains unknown, and a retirement request was mailed to the hospital on the day her trip was scheduled to end."

"The reason for her retirement was stated to be personal reasons... however, there's no evidence that she ever returned home or contacted her family.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1813. ...It'd be harder not to doubt this."
She likely silenced by someone. ...And what's more, it had to have been done by someone with access to police information.
changeReason: New text 'She likely silenced' has grammatical error (missing 'was'). Original is correct.

TextENGNew: It wasn't just Tomoe. Anyone would interpret it that way.
(But... it was such an excessively overt act... so it was a little worrisome.)
Maybe it was setting an example, delivering a message not to dig any deeper.
...But if that's the case, then the culprit clearly doesn't understand Tomoe's way of thinking. She has an intense loathing and desire to fight against people stooping to threats and suppression.
Or maybe they anticipated that, and this was part of a trap to lure her further in? Trying to make her distrust her fellow officers and limit the scope of her investigation activities...?
(Maybe I'm overthinking it.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1816. Plus...)

If she read too deeply into it, she'd only get stuck. ...But it was certainly true that if she indiscriminately disclosed all her official investigation activities, her foes would continue beating her to the punch.
...And so, Tomoe decided to conduct her own investigation on the side, while handling news correspondence and other public relations work.

In light of common sense and discipline, unauthorized activities were never praiseworthy. ...But, even if the project ends with the investigation stalled, ultimately she'd have to take the blame for her big talk.
(So, rather than sitting around and waiting, wouldn't it be better to keep going and accept the blame?)
Although, she wasn't confident that thought was coming from anything but desperation...
"I see.@k@vS14/30/VT4Bb_yamaoki1059. ...So that's the reason you kept this from Yamamura and me?"
"...@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1819.Distrusting your allies is not exactly an admirable trait.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1820. However, there are far too many unusual elements in this investigation."

"There's far too much interference into police activities for this to be just about cleaning up an incident where a male student and a nurse died.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1822. ...That's why I wanted to have my most trusted subordinates look into it with me."
"Hmm... so I didn't meet your qualifications as a trustworthy person?@k@vS14/30/VT4Bb_yamaoki1061. Terribly sorry about that."
"Oh, no... I didn't mean it like that."
This was Yamaoki. If she had asked for his cooperation, he surely would have provided appropriate manpower and personal connections.
...However, if the enemy really was a police officer, that would be taking a big risk. The larger scale their activities, the easier they are to detect.

The same was true for Yamamura, the section chief of the First Division, and for Doujima, section chief of the First Division at prefectural police. Due to those concerns, Tomoe was hesitant to tell them about it until now.
"Still... I'm sorry for making arbitrary assumptions, but I figured you and the others would notice at some point anyhow."
"I hope you can understand why we didn't talk to you about it.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1826. And so, if you're asking about it today... that means something must've happened, I assume?"
"You could say something happened.@k@vS14/30/VT4Bb_yamaoki1063. ...Your trusted subordinate Fujita-kun came and spoke to me."
Yamaoki's smile suddenly vanished and his expression changed. Then he set the documents in his hand aside, leaned slightly forward, and began to speak up.

"He bowed his head to me, asking me to keep this a secret from you.@k@vS14/30/VT4Bb_yamaoki1065. He told me to use whatever reasons I could think of and persuade you to take yourself off of this outside investigation."
"I accepted, and figured I'd check to see if you had any intention of changing your mind."
"I assure you, he didn't betray you.@k@vS14/30/VT4Bb_yamaoki1068. And it's not that he's dissatisfied with the content of the investigation you requested."
"In fact, he said he was deeply worried about you and agonized before making his decision.@k@vS14/30/VT4Bb_yamaoki1070. ...Please try to understand that much."
"...What do you mean by that?"
Though she could understand, she still couldn't wipe away her distrust, so she tried to suppress the feelings in her voice as she asked that.
...Fujita was roughly the same age as her, so he was one of the most friendly and trustworthy detectives within Kakiuchi station.

Though he was a little clumsy and weak-kneed, he came from an athletic background so he put all his heart into his work. He was a good young man with strong convictions and a solid understanding of right and wrong.
So why... why would he try to put a stop to her investigation?

"Don't get the wrong idea, Tomoe-kun.@k@vS14/30/VT4Bb_yamaoki1072. The investigation will still continue, under Fujita-kun, Hanada-kun, and anyone else you trust in the First Division.@k@vS14/30/VT4Bb_yamaoki1073. Yamamura already agreed to take over command."
"Then... why am I being singled out?"

"...@k@vS14/30/VT4Bb_yamaoki1075.Fujita-kun said that if things continue as they are, this case could cost you your life.@k@vS14/30/VT4Bb_yamaoki1076. He's the one acting on-site, so he's likely more sensitive to the atmosphere of the situation.@k@vS14/30/VT4Bb_yamaoki1077. And I also agree with his assessment."

"...There is always risk involved with being a police officer.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1831. Besides, even if you say that, Fujita-kun and his colleagues acting in the field are in far greater danger.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1832. Are you saying it's more acceptable to let those men die?"

"No, you're the one in the greatest danger, Tomoe-kun.@k@vS14/30/VT4Bb_yamaoki1079. If I were in the enemy's shoes, you'd be my first target.@k@vS14/30/VT4Bb_yamaoki1080. ...The fact that you're a woman has nothing to do with it."
Yamaoki continued his explanation, unceremoniously refuting the argument Tomoe brought up.

"If we think of investigators as parts of the human body, they're the arms and legs... That's not belittling them, it's a fact.@k@vS14/30/VT4Bb_yamaoki1082. So, even if they're hurt or lost, they can be replaced."
"...But if the head, the heart, or other vital organs are damaged then the entire body deteriorates, or even shuts down.@k@vS14/30/VT4Bb_yamaoki1084. ...So naturally, those make prime targets."
"...You're thinking too highly of me.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1834. First off, I'm..."

"You're just the chief of news correspondence, on a business trip on behalf of the Public Relations Office.@k@vS14/30/VT4Bb_yamaoki1086. ...It's true that it's just a title, but where do you think investigative HQ would be without you?"

"No matter how strong the gears in a machine are, without proper lubrication they'll eventually rust, and the whole mechanism will halt.@k@vS14/30/VT4Bb_yamaoki1088. And the more complex the inner workings, the more likely that's the case."
...She recalled what Fujita said before.
"You need to get some rest once in a while.@k@vS14/31/VT4Bb_fujita1072. If you collapsed right now, all the NRIPS guys and the people in the field would clash, and this project would disappear."
In fact, even now relationships between the dissatisfied on-scene investigators and the arrogant NRIPS staff who cared nothing for that were already difficult to repair.

In the unlikely event that the project facilitator, Tomoe, were to die or otherwise be removed from the investigation, Section Chief Doujima from the First Division at prefectural police would probably end up completely spoiling relations with NRIPS.
...Thus, some of the people with negative feelings or open opposition to the project had already even slandered Tomoe to upper management.
(...I think they said Deputy Chief Hata was one of them.)

Tomoe was really surprised when she heard it from Public Relations Director Maeno and the informant Arakawa. Surprised that there were people out there who couldn't understand the nature of the police, no matter how old they got...
Be that as it may, Tomoe at least partially understood what sort of role she had to play in her current position.
That's why she was grateful for the concern Yamaoki and the others were showing her... and she felt guilty about it.

"...I truly have great respect for everyone offering their cooperation.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1838. Though they've been very busy and had plenty of reasons to complain, they swallowed it down and continued to support this project of mine.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1839. ...They're too good for me."
"Tomoe-kun..."
"But... no, precisely because of that, I don't want this to end without us getting any closer to the truth."
"NRIPS may treat this as a triumph even if we fail, but it would be squandering the consideration everyone has shown if this case gets closed without us identifying the culprit."

"...We don't have much time left.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1843. Even if it's a bit aggressive, by continuing to work toward the true nature of our enemy we may be able to break out of the deadlock we're in right now.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1844. So..."
"You're willing to risk your life for that...?"
"That's my duty as a police officer.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1846. I'm prepared for the danger. Even if the foe is aiming for me, that makes it all the more convenient."

"That may open the door for this investigation, so someone taking over after me will be able to catch the criminal.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1848. If I have to play the part of the decoy to make that happen, I'll gladly step up."

"And meet the same fate as your father?@k@vS14/30/VT4Bb_yamaoki1092. Two generations sacrificing themselves for the sake of justice.@k@vS14/30/VT4Bb_yamaoki1093. ...I don't see how that helps anyone."
"No, you may still be content with that.@k@vS14/30/VT4Bb_yamaoki1095. Living according to your beliefs, and dying in a gutter."
"Clinging to your convictions as a police officer and passing on with no regrets.@k@vS14/30/VT4Bb_yamaoki1097. ...But, what of the family you leave behind? What of Madoka-kun?"
"Well..."
Tomoe's little sister was precious to her. ...There was no way she hadn't imagined what Madoka's life would be like after her death.
Madoka would be in tears if anything happened to her. ...No, she'd be enraged. And she'd blame herself for her sister's shallow foolishness... and be overcome with grief.

"If this succeeds, if you achieve greatness, I'm sure it'll make them proud to be police officers, and serve as an inspiration to all other police organizations."

"Please forgive me if this is a weird thing to say.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1889. But, the more I feel it... the less I understand what justice truly is."

Tomoe's eyes opened wide as she turned back and nodded, with her hand still on the doorknob.

However.

...Tomoe forgot to even reply and quickly took a seat on the sofa, then desperately suppressed her rising palpitations as she turned toward Yamaoki and leaned forward.

"This may be a recap of modern police history, but let's start with a breakdown of the case.@k@vS14/30/VT4Bb_yamaoki1158. Thirteen years ago, in 1970, a patient being hospitalized for a certain mental illness began behaving erratically. As a result, he jumped off the roof and died immediately..."
"Yes, I know that much."

There was no shortage of material covering the case since it was a highly topical incident that happened in the modern era. In fact, Tomoe was personally interested in the matter so she studied the case outline.

At the time the major ruling party had overwhelming power in the house of representatives, but was on the verge of splitting their majority due to a massive bribery scandal. And the distrust sown from that allowed a newcomer recommended by the opposition party to win the election....
A single patient's death was the beginning of a scandal that shook the political world.
"And, the erratic behavior... included attacking nurses, doctors, and other patients around him, one after another."
"Fortunately, no one died in the incident... At least, that's the official story, but the truth is two patients did die from their wounds."

"According to the report, it was deemed that the social impact would be too large.@k@vS14/30/VT4Bb_yamaoki1162. There was a chance that the general public would begin discriminating against other mentally ill patients... and their actions wouldn't be limited to distancing themselves from them."
"And, when his body was autopsied... they noticed elevated levels of adrenaline in the subject's brain."

Yamaoki nodded to Tomoe, then continued.

"Thanks to that, Lowell Inc was completely banned from selling drugs in Japan."

"And after several years of joint investigations between the police, the Ministry of Health and Welfare, and the Public Prosecutor's Office... the assistant director of the Pharmaceutical Affairs Department at the Ministry of Health and Welfare was arrested."
"As a result, most of the pharmaceutical company executives involved in imports and most of the Pharmaceutical Affairs Board review members of the time were forced to resign."
"Everything after that is just as you heard on the news."

That was bad enough on its own, but the people who were in a position to prevent the problem ignored it in exchange for bribes.

Every day and night, TV news reports were pushing for politicians and businessmen to be held accountable, and newspapers and magazines were detailing the corruption and the lives of all the people involved.
Political distrust bloomed on a massive scale, and an anti-establishment movement led by students was gaining more and more traction...
"But... why were people from Public Security like you and my father involved in the investigation?"

Public Security is not the agency responsible for investigating political or corporate corruption. That would fall more on the Public Prosecutor's Office... under the special investigation department's jurisdiction.

"...What do you think is the greatest enemy of soldiers on the battlefield?"
The question was so sudden and unexpected that for a moment Tomoe couldn't find an answer. ...But after seeing that Yamaoki's expression was completely serious, she thought it over.
"By enemy, do you mean any threatening entity?@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1920. If that's the case, I'd say weapons like nukes, or poison gas..."
"Wrong.@k@vS14/30/VT4Bb_yamaoki1174. ...It's fear."
For any powerful weapon, as long as you know suitable countermeasures or the means to avoid it, it's less of a threat. No matter how overwhelming it may seem, there are ways to neutralize it.
...But once someone succumbs to fear, that can't be undone so easily.
They become terrified of nonexistent enemies and traps.
Or the shock of witnessing death, and an attachment to life.
Either way, soldiers obsessed by their fear completely lose their fighting spirit, so no matter their numbers they're nothing more than a disorderly herd of cattle.
Thus, maintaining the morale of soldiers is critical in times of war.

"And so, if you can eliminate that... then the strongest possible soldier is born.@k@vS14/30/VT4Bb_yamaoki1176. Experience and personality are useful too, but it's most efficient to have their fear physically removed."

"Inebriation with alcohol can also be used to put them in a similar mental state.@k@vS14/30/VT4Bb_yamaoki1178. But if there were a more effective way of accomplishing that, what do you think it might be?"
"Psychotropic drugs?!"
As soon as that thought came to mind, Tomoe gasped.
She heard that drugs and alcohol were being openly used to raise morale during the war.
Indeed, if fear can be eliminated, soldiers will literally give up their lives and rise up to confront any dangerous situation.

"No way... It can't be...?!"
"Believe it or not... we had solid evidence from multiple sources that confirmed it."
"However, even if treatment of mental illness is being used as a pretense, there are very few opportunities to test the effects of these drugs on human subjects."
"And what's more, seeking clinical trials from the general public within the country was far too risky from an ethical perspective.@k@vS14/30/VT4Bb_yamaoki1182. Therefore..."

"Rather than doing it domestically, carry it out overseas... in a country that passes a certain standard of living.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1924. Doing so would make it easier to obtain detailed clinical data similar to the experiments conducted domestically..."
A lesson learned from ancient history... Conquerors have often used the people of their occupied territories for conducting human experiments.

The best way to learn the efficacy of an unknown substance is to actually use it. And it's far easier to understand the results if it's being conducted on a human being, rather than some other animal with unique biology and body structure.
But that fact left a black mark on the history of human society. Even if the drug managed to save the lives of many people, it demanded a hefty sacrifice in exchange.
Thus, it's natural to face public backlash from both a humanitarian and religious standpoint when conducting medical experiments within your own country.
However... what if it's being done "outside" your country? And what if the nature of warfare causes the conquered people to be regarded as inferior...?
The history of pharmacology clearly proves that. And the greatest breakthroughs in medical science came during the Age of Exploration, or the Age of "Conquest"...
"In other words, our own country was engaging with a foreign company... No, it was involved with human experiments happening in foreign countries?"
"...To put it plainly, yes."
"You can't be serious...!@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1928. That's completely unacceptable!!"

"Yes, this is completely serious.@k@vS14/30/VT4Bb_yamaoki1184b. And in order to protect national interests, we needed to put a stop to such a thing.@k@vS14/30/VT4Bb_yamaoki1185. ...That's why we at Public Security were investigating it."
Yamaoki lit another cigarette as he said that.

"Lowell Inc wasn't just doing this for regulated drugs, but also other dangerous drugs that weren't approved for testing in Japan, and taking that data to the head office."

"Then they bribed an intermediary to get it overlooked.@k@vS14/30/VT4Bb_yamaoki1187b. Even though it was an obvious violation of foreign trade control laws and pharmaceutical affairs laws.@k@vS14/30/VT4Bb_yamaoki1188. And the intermediary they used was a businessman with political influence."

"...Unfortunately, we still haven't obtained strong evidence indicating the mastermind behind this.@k@vS14/30/VT4Bb_yamaoki1190. And there is reason to suspect that such unlicensed drugs are being distributed within Japan, even now.@k@vS14/30/VT4Bb_yamaoki1191. Through cash-and-carry wholesalers."
"Cash-and-carry...?"
A cash-and-carry wholesaler is a small to medium size wholesale store that sells goods at lower than market prices, and you can purchase medication there regardless if it's legal or not.

Their sources are quite diverse. Employees from pharmaceutical companies can swipe samples and stock items then exchange them for pocket money, or the vendors can even buy overstocked items from other wholesalers.

In the worst case, the hospitals themselves can be involved in the scheme. A manager or other person in charge secretly sells any excess stock of medication the facility has on hand and lines their pockets with the proceeds.
And some of the drugs being traded there include unlicensed medication that can't be legitimately sold...
"Then... why hasn't it been shut down?"

"The police don't have the authority to do so.@k@vS14/30/VT4Bb_yamaoki1192b. And of course, the same goes for Public Security.@k@vS14/30/VT4Bb_yamaoki1193. The Ministry of Health and Welfare manages pharmaceutical affairs, and the Public Prosecutor's Office is responsible for corruption in the political and business world."
"And, the odds of them being cordial enough to cooperate are approximately... no, pretty much zero."
"But I think that Yuusuke... your father managed to grab onto something.@k@vS14/30/VT4Bb_yamaoki1196. Because otherwise, they wouldn't have so overtly silenced him."
"...@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1934.Does that have anything to do with why you brought me to Kakiuchi?"
For the longest time... any time she asked that question, he'd dodge it.
For a high ranking member of Public Security like Yamaoki, accepting a chief position at a local precinct was like taking an early retirement.

And so... she wanted to know. She wanted to hear the real motivation, straight from Yamaoki's mouth...
Yamaoki took two or three more smokes... remaining quiet the whole time.
Normally, the conversation would end right there.
However.
"...It does."
For the first time... for the very first time, Yamaoki said it... as he turned to Tomoe and nodded.
As soon as she heard that... she lost the ability to stand. Her whole body stiffened as all the strength drained from her.
In the end... she was right.
Her speculation turned out to be completely true...!

It seems he had connections to a certain political group thanks to the citizens group campaign... and Public Security came to the conclusion it was connected with CIRO."

Though all her doubts about why she was assigned a position in such an unfamiliar location had been eliminated... Tomoe was now worried about a new question that arose from that information.

...One month left.
After that, Tomoe would be called back to the National Police Agency's Public Relations Office to start on another task.

She couldn't be satisfied with that. There was no sense of accomplishment, as if she'd been involved in a major scam.

But... the more grateful she was for the support and expectations of her colleagues, the more frustrated she got when she found herself getting stuck like this.
If she wanted to keep pursuing her personal justice, she had to keep moving forward in accordance with her beliefs.
Accepting the collateral and retribution alone, making sure nobody else got hurt and dragged down.

Is it right to brush that off and stick to your beliefs? Or are you just doing it out of self-complacency...?
"...Anyway, I hope things turn out well."
"About the medicine.@k@vS14/32/VT4Bb_hanada1052. We received a sample from Natsumi Kimiyoshi, so if the analysis goes as you predicted we should be able to make progress on the investigation soon."
"...Right."
Matsuoka from the forensics team at Gifu prefectural station was an old acquaintance of Tomoe's from college. He was highly skilled, and she could rely on him to keep things confidential.

(If the medication had any kind of effect on her mind, then the investigation will move forward significantly.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1945. It might even give us grounds to conduct an investigation of the university hospital in Gogura.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1946. ...But.)
Somehow, she couldn't wipe away her listless thoughts. It wasn't enough to raise her spirits.

"Haah... Huh...?"
As she sighed and turned toward the window, she suddenly caught a glimpse of a sign that read "Kakiuchi service area, 1 km ahead."

"Anyway, it looks like there's a lot of traffic in the city area up ahead... so maybe a short break would be for the best.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1956. What do you say?"

"Alright, it'll only be a moment.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1958. I'll be right back."
"Oh, in that case I'll fill up the tank.@k@vS14/32/VT4Bb_hanada1059. I'll park near the bathrooms, so meet me over there in about 10 minutes."
"...I told you, I'm not here for the bathroom!@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1960. I'm gonna clobber you!"
It was Sunday today, so the area in front of the shop was crowded with families and couples. To escape the crowds, Tomoe headed to a vending machine off in a shady corner.

"Ouch... is this served hot?!"
Tomoe cursed at the vending machine while gently picking it up with her fingers. Well, it was her mistake for pressing the button without checking first, but who sells hot drinks in the summer...?
(Try reading the air, damn it.)

It would be a waste to buy another, so she rolled it back and forth in the palm of her hand to cool it off. ...Tomoe let out a sigh, wondering if she should've just gotten some free cold water from the shop.
"Haah...... Huh?"
She suddenly turned toward the parking lot, feeling a strange sense that something was out of place... no, it was more like deja vu, so she quickly sharpened her vision.
She had visited the Kakiuchi service area many times in the past... but there was something different about the scene stretched out in front of her.
...Some time in the past... after finishing up an errand... she came here.

She sure of it... Hanada had driven her here before.

I found the address plate posted on the corner and made sure the letters matched up exactly as I heard them... then let out a sigh.

The asphalt looked recently paved and had a nice smooth surface. And the neighborhood association must be doing a good job cleaning up, because there was no trash to be found anywhere.
It was like a scene cut right out of television... Like a completely different world.

My knees had no strength left in them, and my legs felt like they were being crushed.

...This is....
This is the house where Nagisa Ozaki-chan lives.

I'd be bothering them if I showed up without letting them know ahead of time... Just how many times did I start to make that excuse before swallowing it down?
However.
This is something I have to do for myself to set things right.
I pressed the doorbell.
...They might not be home. Or maybe the bell is broken.

"...Umm, is this... the home of... Ozaki-san?"

"Long time no see"?

"......@k@vS14/35/VT4Bb_nagisa1010.Reina."

"...I'd like to meet you.@k@vS14/35/VT4Bb_nagisa1012. Would you like to meet up somewhere?"

"Sorry.@k@vS14/35/VT4Bb_nagisa1014. There are a lot of things... so many things to talk about, and I can't speak well over the phone.@k@vS14/35/VT4Bb_nagisa1015. Reina, I want to meet you... and talk with you.@k@vS14/35/VT4Bb_nagisa1016. I want to see your face and talk with you again..."
"Nagisa-chan..."

"Then I'll head up your way next Sunday.@k@vS14/35/VT4Bb_nagisa1025. Can you tell me some place we should meet?"
"N-no way... I-I should be the one coming to you!@k@vS14/02/VT4Bb_rena1027. Nagisa-chan, I can't make you go to the trouble of coming all the way here..."

"It's fine.@k@vS14/35/VT4Bb_nagisa1027. It's for your sake too, Reina. I'm sure you'd be more comfortable meeting and talking at a place you know, right?@k@vS14/35/VT4Bb_nagisa1028. ...So, please.@k@vS14/35/VT4Bb_nagisa1029. Let me do this."
"...Nagisa-chan."

However, when the scheduled day arrived.
Nagisa-chan... didn't come.

"...Good evening."

"Long time no see.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1994. It's been... about half a year?"

"In Hinamizawa.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe1999. And also in Kakiuchi."
"...@k@vS14/02/VT4Bb_rena1040.But... this is the first time Rena ever came to this town."

Suddenly.
